Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Substitute Successor For Deceased Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jul-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO SUBSTITUTE AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST FOR DECEASED PLAINTIFF

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for an order to substitute Peter Krysinski as successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff, Theodore Krysinski, is granted. CCP 377.32(a)(5)(B) authorizes Peter Krysinski to act on behalf of Arlene Krysinski. The court is not ruling on whether the substitution of Peter renders the survival claim(s) time-barred. Any defendant is free to raise that issue once, by appropriate motion or at trial, once an order is signed permitting Peter to act on behalf of Arlene to pursue the survival claim(s). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Substitute Successor For Deceased Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jul-19-2011 Continued To Jul-26-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 220
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to substitute successor for deceased plaintiff is continued on the court's own motion to July 26, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 to give defendant Kerr Corporation an opportunity, if it wishes, to provide any supplemental opposition to the motion in light of the submission of the authorization signed by Arlene Krysinski with the reply papers. The authorization, which was signed prior to the filing of the motion, should have been included with the moving papers. Kerr Corporation may file supplemental opposition papers no later than July 22. No further papers may be filed on this motion. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
